title: The Emperor's Favor
content:
After the banquet, Li Wei's relationship with the Emperor grew even closer. He began to spend more time with her, often inviting her to his study for tea and conversation.

One afternoon, the Emperor surprised Li Wei by taking her to the palace's private garden.

"I wanted to show you this," the Emperor said, walking through the garden.

Li Wei followed him, her heart racing. The garden was beautiful, with flowers of every color and a small pond in the center.

"This is my favorite place in the palace," the Emperor said. "I come here when I need to think."

He stopped at the pond and looked at Li Wei. "And now I want to share it with you."

Li Wei felt a warmth in her chest. "Thank you, Your Majesty."

The Emperor smiled. "You don't have to call me 'Your Majesty' when we're alone. Call me by my name - Zhao Yi."

Li Wei hesitated, then spoke. "Zhao Yi."

The Emperor smiled. "There. That's better."

They sat by the pond for a while, talking about their lives. The Emperor told Li Wei about his childhood, about his struggles to become Emperor. Li Wei told him about her father, about her dreams for the future.

As the sun set, the Emperor took Li Wei's hand.

"Li Wei," he said, his voice soft. "I... I care about you."

Li Wei looked into his eyes. She could see the sincerity in them, and for a moment, she forgot about the palace, about the dangers, about everything.

"I care about you too," she said.

The Emperor smiled, and he kissed her.

***

That night, Li Wei couldn't sleep. She kept thinking about the Emperor's kiss, about the way he had looked at her. She knew that this relationship was dangerous, but she couldn't help how she felt.

The next morning, Li Wei woke up to find that the palace was abuzz with gossip. Everyone knew about her and the Emperor.

"Have you heard?" one servant said. "Lady Li spent the night with the Emperor!"

"Impossible!" another said. "The Empress will never allow it!"

Li Wei ignored the gossip, but she knew that it would only get worse. The Empress would not tolerate this.

Sure enough, later that day, the Empress summoned Li Wei.

"Lady Li," the Empress said, her voice cold. "I've heard about your... relationship with the Emperor."

Li Wei stood up, her back straight. "I don't know what you're talking about, Empress."

The Empress laughed. "Don't play dumb, Lady Li. Everyone in the palace knows what happened."

She stood up and walked to Li Wei, her eyes filled with anger.

"Let me make this clear," the Empress said. "You will stay away from the Emperor. If I catch you with him again, there will be consequences."

Li Wei looked at the Empress, her eyes calm. "I'm sorry, Empress, but I can't do that."

The Empress's face turned red with anger. "You dare to defy me?"

"I dare to follow my heart," Li Wei said.

The Empress slapped her.

"How dare you!" the Empress said. "You are nothing but a lowly concubine, and you think you can challenge me?"

Li Wei held her cheek, her eyes filled with tears. But she did not back down.

"I may be a concubine," she said, her voice steady. "But I am also a human being, and I have feelings."

She turned and walked away, leaving the Empress standing alone.

***

That night, Li Wei went to see the Emperor.

"Zhao Yi," she said, walking into his study.

The Emperor looked up, surprised. "Li Wei? What's wrong?"

Li Wei told him about the Empress's threat.

The Emperor's face turned dark. "I'll talk to her."

"No," Li Wei said. "I don't want you to get involved."

The Emperor stood up and walked to her. "You're my responsibility, Li Wei. I won't let anyone hurt you."

He took her in his arms, and Li Wei felt a sense of safety. She knew that with the Emperor by her side, she could face anything.

But she also knew that this relationship would only make things more dangerous. The Empress would not give up easily, and Li Wei would have to be prepared for whatever came next.

The game was getting more complicated, and Li Wei was determined to win.
